summary: Header/type hygiene PR that moved item state/logic table definitions out of the broad `src/melee/it/types.h` header and into the item-specific `src/melee/it/items/types.h`. The diff updated many item headers to include `it/items/types.h` for `ItemStateTable`/`ItemLogicTable` while adding `it/forward.h` where only `Item_GObj`-style forward declarations were needed. It also moved several `ItemLogicTable` extern declarations from `item.c` into `it_2725.h`. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the provided context, so intent beyond dependency cleanup is inferred from the title and mechanical include changes.
what_changed: Added `struct ItemStateTable` to `src/melee/it/items/types.h`, preserving its fields: `anim_id`, `animated`, `physics_updated`, and `collided`.

Added `struct ItemLogicTable` to `src/melee/it/items/types.h`, preserving its item callback table fields such as `states`, `spawned`, `destroyed`, `picked_up`, `dropped`, `thrown`, `dmg_dealt`, `dmg_received`, `entered_air`, `reflected`, `clanked`, `absorbed`, `shield_bounced`, `hit_shield`, and `evt_unk`.
Removed the same `ItemStateTable` and `ItemLogicTable` struct definitions from `src/melee/it/types.h`.
Added `ItemLogicTable` and `ItemStateTable` forward typedefs to `src/melee/it/items/forward.h` and removed them from the broader `src/melee/it/forward.h`.

Updated many `src/melee/it/items/*.h` headers to replace `#include "it/types.h"` with `#include "it/items/types.h"`, usually adding `#include "it/forward.h"` to keep `Item_GObj` and related item forward declarations available.
Changed `src/melee/it/items/types.h` to export `it/items/forward.h` with an IWYU pragma and to include `<baselib/forward.h>` for HSD callback typedefs used by the moved structs.
Added `#include "it/items/forward.h"` to `src/melee/it/types.h`, keeping broad item type consumers able to see item-table forward declarations without keeping the full table definitions there.

Moved extern declarations for `it_803F14C4`, `it_803F23CC`, `it_803F3100`, and `it_803F4D20` from local declarations in `src/melee/it/item.c` into `src/melee/it/it_2725.h`, and included that header from `item.c`.

Adjusted fighter source includes in `ftNs_AttackHi4.c` and `ftSk_SpecialS.c`, removing `it/forward.h` from the top include block and adding `it/types.h` near the item headers where full item types were needed.
smart_moves: Kept the move mechanical: the structs were relocated with their documented offsets and field names intact rather than renamed or reshaped, lowering matching/regression risk.

Separated ownership of forward declarations from full type definitions: `it/items/forward.h` now owns `ItemStateTable` and `ItemLogicTable` forward typedefs, while `it/items/types.h` owns their layouts.
Used `it/forward.h` plus `it/items/types.h` in item headers instead of relying on the heavy umbrella `it/types.h`, reducing accidental transitive dependencies.
Added the needed `<baselib/forward.h>` include at the destination because the moved structs reference HSD callback types such as `HSD_GObjPredicate`, `HSD_GObjEvent`, and `HSD_GObjInteraction`.
Centralized `ItemLogicTable` extern declarations in a header (`it_2725.h`) instead of leaving them as ad hoc externs inside `item.c`, making table symbols reusable and easier to audit.
decomp_lessons: When a type is primarily consumed by per-item headers, put the full struct layout in the item subdirectory header rather than the broad subsystem `types.h`.

After moving a struct, audit every consumer for whether it needs the full definition or only a forward declaration; this PR repeatedly pairs `it/forward.h` with `it/items/types.h` to make that dependency explicit.

Do not leave relocated table symbols as local `extern` declarations in a C file if they are part of a shared item interface; declare them in the relevant header with their known array sizes when available.
Preserve offset comments and `@todo Size unknown` notes during header moves so future struct-size and field-layout work does not lose existing decomp evidence.
Include-order cleanup can affect non-item systems too: fighter files that instantiate or inspect item data may need `it/types.h` even if they no longer directly include `it/forward.h`.
A broad type header can still re-export forwards from a narrower header to maintain compatibility while moving heavy definitions out of the umbrella include.
naming_conventions: Item table types use PascalCase names: `ItemStateTable` and `ItemLogicTable`.

Per-item headers live under `src/melee/it/items/` and commonly use lower-case item names such as `itbombhei.h`, `itbox.h`, `itfoods.h`, plus address-style unknown headers such as `it_27CF.h` and `it_2F28.h`.
Item data table externs retain linker/address-style names such as `it_803F14C4`, `it_803F23CC`, `it_803F3100`, and `it_803F4D20`, with address comments like `/* 3F14C4 */`.

Struct fields in `ItemLogicTable` are named after lifecycle/callback roles: `spawned`, `destroyed`, `picked_up`, `dropped`, `thrown`, `dmg_dealt`, `dmg_received`, `entered_air`, `reflected`, `clanked`, `absorbed`, `shield_bounced`, `hit_shield`, and `evt_unk`.
The moved structs keep decomp annotation style with `/// @at{...} @sz{4}` field comments.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-specific matching tactic is described in PR text or review comments.
The main matching-safety tactic evident from the diff is preserving struct layouts and field ordering exactly while moving headers, avoiding changes to generated code semantics.
Replacing umbrella includes with precise forward/type includes can reduce compile-order and dependency noise without touching function bodies.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review body text were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue moving item-specific structs from `it/types.h` into `it/items/types.h` or per-item headers.
Search for remaining item headers that include `it/types.h` only to get `ItemStateTable` or `ItemLogicTable`.
Audit whether `ItemLogicTable` size is now known and whether the `@todo Size unknown` on `ItemStateTable` was later resolved.
Search for later changes to `it_803F14C4`, `it_803F23CC`, `it_803F3100`, or `it_803F4D20` declarations and array sizes.
Check whether similar forward/type split conventions were applied to fighter, stage, or effect subsystem headers.
